element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Scoring penalty system; players must avoid killing hostages in crossfire
Resource management and tactical weapon selection
Bonus level transition; originally planned with enemy encounters
Multi-phase boss with turret destruction followed by direct combat

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Frantic action with sustained enemy pressure; two-player simultaneous play intensifies combat density

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden developer messages encoded as powerup letters spelling 'SNK' and 'KNT' in Stage 3
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hostages placed in high-traffic enemy areas create risk-reward tension

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat mechanics and hostage rescue mechanics with light enemy pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tank sequences and weapon variety; increased enemy density and coordination
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained multi-wave encounters culminating in complex boss patterns
notable_spikes: Stage 10 final boss: Two-phase encounter with turret destruction prerequisite
Multi-player sprite overload causing occasional powerup failure to spawn in Stage 3

notable_level_patterns.description: Enemy-dense areas containing civilian hostages; scoring system penalizes collateral damage, creating risk-reward tension between speed and precision
Tank operation sequences breaking up infantry combat; originally designed with more complex routing before simplification
Minecart sequence marking progression to second half; appears simplified from arcade version which had multiple rail routes and soldier encounters
Staged boss arena with destructible environmental threats preceding direct character combat; final phase shows dictator fleeing (unused graphic suggests alternative ending progression)

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Guerrilla War (NES) technical documentation
wikipedia - Guerrilla War (video game) release and design overview
